avalancheActivity.comment: Fresh snowdrifts can trigger medium-sized avalanches even by minimum additional loading. Danger zones occur primarily in steep ridgeline terrain and in wind-loaded gullies and bowls. Frequency and spread of avalanche prone locations increase with ascending altitude. If avalanches on high-altitude shady slopes fracture down to deeper weak layers, they can in isolated cases then grow to large size. Such avalanche prone locations occur primarily in spots where the snow is shallow or in transitions from shallow to deep snow.

snowpackStructure.comment: The most recent fresh snow from the weekend has already bonded well with the widespread melt-freeze crusts. Fresh snowdrifts have been generated near ridgelines. The mid-level part of the snowpack consists of well-settled, compact layers widespread which on high-altitude shady slopes cover a poorly structured old snowpack fundament.

tendency.highlights: Avalanche danger will continue to slowly diminish overall, but freshly generated snowdrift accumulations require caution.

weatherForecast.comment: During the nighttime hours, heavy cloud cover and some showers passing through from Bregenzerwald to Arlberg and Montafon. Nocturnal snow showers will swiftly taper off, during the morning hours the clouds will gradually disperse. Intermittent sunshine is possible. Temperature at 2000m: -6  to -4 degrees; at 3000m: -10 degrees. Moderate to brisk N/NE winds at high altitudes.

avalancheActivity.comment: Fresh small-sized snowdrift accumulations are prone to triggering. Danger zones occur primarily on steep ridgeline slopes and in wind-loaded gullies and bowls. Frequency and spread of avalanche prone locations increase with ascending altitude. In addition, in avalanches on shady slopes fracture down to deeper layers inside the snowpqck they can in isolated cases grow to large size. Such danger zones occur primarily in spots where the snow is shallow and in transitions from shallow to deep snow.

tendency.highlights: Avalanche danger is not expected to change significantly. Caution urged towards fresh snowdrifts.

weatherForecast.comment: During the nighttime hours, heavy cloud cover and some showers passing through from Bregenzerwald to Arlberg and Montafon. Nocturnal snow showers will swiftly taper off, during the morning hours the clouds will gradually disperse. Intermittent sunshine is possible. Temperature at 2000m: -6  to -4 degrees; at 3000m: -10 degrees. Moderate to brisk N/NE winds at high altitudes.

avalancheActivity.comment: Avalanche danger above the timberline is moderate, below that altitude danger is low. Fresh snowdrift accumulations require caution. Danger zones occur primarily in steep ridgeline terrain and in wind-loaded gullies and bowls. If avalanches on high-altitude shady slopes fracture down to deeper weak layers, they can in isolated cases then grow to larger size. Apart from the risks of being buried in snow masses, the danger of being swept along and forced to take a fall require caution.
avalancheActivity.highlights: Beware small fresh snowdrifts: prone to triggering

snowpackStructure.comment: The most recent fresh snow from the weekend has already bonded well with the widespread melt-freeze crusts. Fresh snowdrifts have been generated near ridgelines. The mid-level part of the snowpack consists of well-settled, compact layers widespread which on high-altitude shady slopes cover a poorly structured old snowpack fundament.

tendency.highlights: Avalanche danger will continue to diminish gradually.

Nocturnal snow showers will swiftly taper off, during the morning hours the clouds will gradually disperse. Intermittent sunshine is possible. Temperature at 2000m: -6  to -4 degrees; at 3000m: -10 degrees. Moderate to brisk N/NE winds at high altitudes.
